title: The Washing of the Feet
subtitle: Christ rises from supper and stoops to wash the feet of his disciples

stillness: Be still now, and attend diligently.
According to Chrysostom, learn the silence of true humility.
Let your soul grow quiet before the One who stoops.
gospel: Before the feast of the Passover,
Jesus, knowing that his hour was come,
that he should pass out of this world to the Father:
having loved his own who were in the world,
he loved them unto the end.
the devil having now put it into the heart of Judas,
the son of Simon Iscariot, to betray him;
knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands,
and that he came from God and goeth to God:
he riseth from supper, and layeth aside his garments,
and having taken a towel, girded himself.
Then he poureth water into a basin,
and began to wash the feet of the disciples,
and to wipe them with the towel wherewith he was girded.
He cometh therefore to Simon Peter.
Lord, dost thou wash my feet?
behold: Attend to each thing as if you were present.
Behold the Lord Jesus, King of kings and Lord of lords,
who did not forget that he was the natural Son of God,
and yet, knowing himself to be such,
fulfils not the office of God and Lord, but of a man and a servant.
Behold: he rises from the table.
With his own hands, not by the service of others,
he takes the towel and girds himself.
He pours water into a basin of stone,
and so lovingly, so honourably, so dutifully made ready,
he begins to wash the muddy feet of the disciples,
and to wipe them with the towel wherewith he was girded.
See how he treads down all swelling pride,
when he who comes from God and goes to God
willed to wash the feet of men.
consider: Consider: if he who was the Son of God, equal to the Father,
bowed down his majesty from God
to humble himself to the feet of a man,
what ought a wretched man to do, who is earth and ashes?
The devil does not implant evil thoughts, but enkindles them;
he cannot cast a man down unless the man casts himself down
and hands his weapons back to the enemy by consenting.
Behold Peter, terrified at the divinity and majesty of Christ,
wholly astonished, refusing what seemed to him unfitting:
Thou the Lord, I the servant;
thou the Creator, I the creature.
And dost thou, Lord, wash the feet of me,
a wretched, most poor, most lowly fisherman,
with these hands by which thou didst open the eyes of the blind,
cleanse the lepers, and raise the dead?
The more we are great, the more let us be humbled in all things,
and never grow proud of the gifts of God.
prayer: O Jesus, gentle example of true humility,
who didst wash the feet of thy disciples,
I ask and beseech thee, Lord, purge my affections,
that, with both feet — that is, with the twofold love —
I may come secure to thee, my purifier.
Purify me unto the end of my days,
and cleanse me from every stain of sin,
that, my negligences and sins forgiven,
the enemies who watch my heel
may fall back confounded on the day of my death.
Direct, O Lord, my feet into the way of peace,
that, delivered from the hands of all my enemies,
I may bless thee with all thine elect,
unto the ages of ages. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Ex quo universum conculcat tumorem, dum is qui exit a Deo, et ad Deum vadit, pedes lavare voluit.
latinJewel.english: By this he treads down all swelling pride, when he who comes from God and goes to God willed to wash men's feet.
